name: Corned Beef Coronets
recipeInstructions:
If using your own coleslaw recipe, make it extra thick to avoid drips. If using prepared coleslaw, drain off excess liquid before use. For an especially colorful effect, use shredded red cabbage for your coleslaw.
1. Combine the coleslaw and breadcrumbs thoroughly.
2. Spread each corned beef slice with a light layer of mustard.
3. Place about 1 teaspoon of the coleslaw-breadcrumb mixture on top of each slice.
4. Distribute the filling evenly over the corned beef.
5. Roll the corned beef around the coleslaw to form neat coronets (logs).
6. Secure each coronet with a cellophane-tipped toothpick and refrigerate until serving time.
Makes about 30 coronets.
From The Big Beautiful Book of Hors d'Oeuvres by Julia Weinberg.
